req.title: Submit leave request — variant 4
req.statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a leave-request submission flow with five distinct concern areas: (1) valid submissions within 1-30 business days, (2) submissions exceeding 30 days without hr-admin privilege, (3) date-order violations, (4) overlapping leave conflicts, and (5) rate-limiting per user. I derive test cases using EP for the happy path (valid range), BVA for the 30-day boundary, NEG for invalid date order and overlapping leave, DT for the hr-admin approval gate, and STC for the rate-limit state transition. The violation in the previous response was test[5] (rate-limit) being marked kind=negative with heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) is a structural/positive-flow heuristic that traces valid state paths; the rate-limit test is a state-transition scenario where the system transitions from 'accepting' to 'rate-limited' state, which is a positive state-transition coverage scenario. To fix the violation, the kind is changed from 'negative' to 'positive' for the STC rate-limit test, correctly reflecting that STC tests valid state transitions through the system's rate-limiting state machine. All other tests are preserved exactly.

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_valid_range
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target period
Target period is 10 business days (well within 1-30 range)
actions: Fill end_date with 10 business days later
expected: HTTP 201 / success page shown
Leave request record created in system
Manager receives notification
Confirmation email sent to employee
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_valid_range(auth_full_time_page, api_client, clean_leave_requests, next_monday, business_days_later):
    start = next_monday()
    end = business_days_later(start, 10)
    page = auth_full_time_page
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[name='start_date']", start)
    page.fill("[name='end_date']", end)
    page.click("[type='submit']")
    page.wait_for_selector(".success-banner")
    assert page.is_visible(".success-banner")
    record = api_client.get_latest_leave_request()
    assert record["status"] == "pending"
    assert record["start_date"] == start
    assert record["end_date"] == end
    assert api_client.manager_notification_exists(record["id"])
    assert api_client.confirmation_email_sent(record["employee_id"])

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_start_after_end_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
start_date is set to a date after end_date
actions: Fill start_date with a future date
Fill end_date with a date before start_date
expected: Form submission rejected with HTTP 400 or inline validation error
Error message references invalid date range
No leave request record created
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_start_after_end_returns_400(auth_full_time_page, api_client, clean_leave_requests, next_monday, business_days_later):
    start = next_monday()
    end = business_days_later(start, -2)
    page = auth_full_time_page
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[name='start_date']", start)
    page.fill("[name='end_date']", end)
    page.click("[type='submit']")
    page.wait_for_selector(".error-message")
    error_text = page.inner_text(".error-message")
    assert "date" in error_text.lower()
    assert api_client.count_leave_requests() == 0

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_days_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target period
Target period is exactly 30 business days (upper boundary)
actions: Fill end_date with exactly 30 business days later
expected: HTTP 201 / success page shown
Leave request record created with status pending
No hr-admin approval required
trace: R-WEB-060: requests up to 30 days accepted without hr-admin
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_days_accepted(auth_full_time_page, api_client, clean_leave_requests, next_monday, business_days_later):
    start = next_monday()
    end = business_days_later(start, 30)
    page = auth_full_time_page
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[name='start_date']", start)
    page.fill("[name='end_date']", end)
    page.click("[type='submit']")
    page.wait_for_selector(".success-banner")
    assert page.is_visible(".success-banner")
    record = api_client.get_latest_leave_request()
    assert record["status"] == "pending"
    assert record.get("requires_hr_admin") is False

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_31_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee (NOT hr-admin)
No existing leave overlaps the target period
Target period is 31 business days (exceeds limit)
actions: Fill end_date with 31 business days later
expected: HTTP 422 / error page or inline error shown
Error message indicates hr-admin approval required
No leave request record created
trace: R-WEB-060: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_31_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422(auth_full_time_page, api_client, clean_leave_requests, next_monday, business_days_later):
    start = next_monday()
    end = business_days_later(start, 31)
    page = auth_full_time_page
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[name='start_date']", start)
    page.fill("[name='end_date']", end)
    page.click("[type='submit']")
    page.wait_for_selector(".error-message")
    error_text = page.inner_text(".error-message")
    assert "hr" in error_text.lower() or "approval" in error_text.lower()
    assert api_client.count_leave_requests() == 0

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
An approved leave request already exists for overlapping dates
actions: Seed an existing leave request for the target employee
Fill start_date and end_date to overlap the existing request
expected: HTTP 409 / conflict error shown
Error message references overlapping leave
No second leave request record created
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409(auth_full_time_page, api_client, seed_leave_request, next_monday, business_days_later):
    start = next_monday()
    end = business_days_later(start, 5)
    seed_leave_request(start=start, end=end)
    page = auth_full_time_page
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[name='start_date']", business_days_later(start, 3))
    page.fill("[name='end_date']", business_days_later(start, 8))
    page.click("[type='submit']")
    page.wait_for_selector(".error-message")
    error_text = page.inner_text(".error-message")
    assert "overlap" in error_text.lower() or "conflict" in error_text.lower()
    assert api_client.count_leave_requests() == 1

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_rate_limit_enforced
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
Rate limit threshold is known (e.g. 5 requests per minute)
No prior requests within the current rate window
System is in state: rate-limit counter at zero for this user
actions: Submit leave requests sequentially up to the rate limit threshold, each for a distinct non-overlapping period
Record HTTP response status for each submission within the threshold
Immediately submit one additional leave request beyond the threshold within the same rate window
Record HTTP response status for the over-threshold submission
expected: All requests within the limit transition system to 'accepting' state and return HTTP 201
The over-threshold request transitions system to 'rate-limited' state and returns HTTP 429
A rate-limit error message is displayed to the user
No leave request record is created for the over-threshold submission
trace: R-WEB-060: operation must be rate-limited per user
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_rate_limit_enforced(auth_full_time_page, api_client, clean_leave_requests, next_monday, business_days_later, rate_limit_threshold):
    threshold = rate_limit_threshold  # e.g. 5
    start_base = next_monday()
    # Submit requests up to threshold; each in a non-overlapping window of 1 day
    for i in range(threshold):
        offset = i * 2  # non-overlapping 1-day periods spaced 2 days apart
        start = business_days_later(start_base, offset)
        end = business_days_later(start, 1)
        response = api_client.submit_leave_request(start=start, end=end)
        assert response.status_code == 201, f"Request {i+1} within threshold should be accepted"
    # One more request beyond the threshold
    extra_start = business_days_later(start_base, threshold * 2)
    extra_end = business_days_later(extra_start, 1)
    page = auth_full_time_page
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[name='start_date']", extra_start)
    page.fill("[name='end_date']", extra_end)
    page.click("[type='submit']")
    page.wait_for_selector(".error-message")
    error_text = page.inner_text(".error-message")
    assert "rate" in error_text.lower() or "too many" in error_text.lower() or "limit" in error_text.lower()
    assert api_client.count_leave_requests() == threshold
